sanskrit:
कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिम् आस्थिता जनकादयः।
लोकसंग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन् कर्तुम् अर्हसि॥
transliteration:
karmaṇaiva hi saṁsiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ |
lokasaṅgraham evāpi sampaśyan kartum arhasi ||
literal_meaning: By action alone indeed perfection was attained by Janaka and others; considering the welfare of the world, you too should act.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reminds Arjuna that enlightened role-models continued to act not from personal need but to model healthy functioning for society, reframing duty as compassionate social modeling rather than burdensome obligation.
psychological_state: Arjuna shifts from paralysis-by-example ("I’m not perfect like Janaka") to empowerment-through-example ("My action itself can guide others").
trigger_scenario: A seasoned therapist recovering from burnout who questions returning to practice; remembering that clients benefit simply from witnessing an authentic, values-based life convinces her to resume.
anchor_text: Act selflessly to guide others, as wise leaders do.
